summary: iOS 👀 | 5200 | 4320 | DFU is NOT blocked during the call
scenario: DFU firmware update is not blocked during an active softphone call on Poly Studio Mobile app
description_clean: DFU (firmware update) is not blocked during an active call. When a user is on a softphone call (e.g., Zoom) and navigates to Device Settings > Software to tap Update, they are taken to the Software Update screen and allowed to trigger DFU. Expected behavior: a popup message should appear informing the user that DFU is not available during a call.
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Connect to a supported Poly Bluetooth audio device.
3. Initiate an active softphone call (e.g., Zoom, Teams, etc.) using the connected Poly device.
4. While the call is active, navigate to the Home screen in the Poly Studio Mobile app.
5. Tap on the connected [Device Name] to open Device Settings.
6. Scroll to and tap 'Software' under Device Settings.
7. Tap 'Update' on the Software Update screen.
8. Observe whether the DFU (firmware update) process is allowed to proceed.
expected: A popup message should appear informing the user that DFU is not available during an active call, and the firmware update should be blocked.
actual: No popup is shown; the user is taken to the Software Update screen and allowed to trigger DFU even while on an active call.
